W1.descripcion_general: W1 is for archers with moderately affected coordination, highly affected range of movement, or the absence of limbs. These archers all shoot from seated and create their own style in pulling the bow back and aiming.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination moderately affected throughout the whole body.
Movement and coordination affected to a high degree on one side of the body, one arm and both legs.
Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs, with one arm also moderately affected.
Movement and coordination moderately affected in the trunk and arms and highly affected in the hands and legs.
Movement affected to a high degree in the trunk, legs and hands with movement moderately affected in the arms.

W2.descripcion_general: W2 is for archers who have movement highly affected in the body and legsThese archers all shoot from seated and and generate power in the bow mainly from the upper back, shoulders and arms.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement in both legs affected to a high degree.
Movement in the lower trunk and legs affected to a high degree.
Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs.
Movement highly affected in the body below the chest and in the legs.
Movement in the legs highly affected and mildly affected in the trunk.

Standing.descripcion_general: Standing archers have movement affected in the legs or the absence of arms.  All are able to stand but some may choose to shot from sitting to draw the bow using their feet.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement and coordination mildly affected in the legs.
The movement of one leg affected to a high degree.
The absence of both arms.
Absence of  both legs below the knee.
Absence of legs from mid-thigh.

Open.descripcion_general: Open is for archers with moderately affected movement legs or hips, highly affected in the legs and trunk, or the absence of limbs. They can shoot from sitting or resting on a stool for balance.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination affected in the legs to a moderate degree.
Movement of the hip joint moderately affected.
The movement of one leg affected to a high degree.
Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs.
The absence of both lower legs.

W1 Doubles.descripcion_general: W1 Doubles is for archers with moderately affected coordination, highly affected range of movement, or the absence of limbs.These archers all shoot from seated and create their own style in pulling the bow back and aiming.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination moderately affected throughout the whole body.
Movement affected to a high degree in the body and legs, mildly affected in the arms with hand grip and control highly affected.
Movement and coordination affected to a high degree on one side of the body, one arm and both legs.
The absence of both arms.
The absence of both forearms.

Open Doubles.descripcion_general: Open Doubles is for archers with moderately affected movement legs or hips, highly affected in the legs and trunk, or the absence of limbs.They can shoot from sitting or resting on a stool for balance, with one player from W2 and one from the Standing class.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement in both legs affected to a high degree.
Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs.
Movement in the legs highly affected and mildly affected in the trunk.
Movement and coordination mildly affected in the legs.
The absence of both arms.
Absence of legs from mid-thigh.

V1.descripcion_general: V1 is for archers with a near-total visual impairment.All shoot wearing a blindfold.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Near-total visual impairment.

V2/3.descripcion_general: V2/3 is for archers with a visual impairment.They can see up to a distance of five metres and have a maximum field of view of forty degrees.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderate visual impairment, able to see approximately one metre.
A visual impairment, able to see a distance approximately five metres.

T11.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT11 is for long jumpers with a near-total visual impairment. All compete in blacked-out glasses. A sighted guide assists with run-up direction and take-off timing.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Near-total visual impairment.

T12.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT12 is for long jumpers with a visual impairment.They can recognise a moving object at a distance of roughly one metre. They count strides to hit the take-off board correctly.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderate visual impairment, able to see approximately one metre.

T12 (T11/12).descripcion_general: T12 is for runners with a visual impairment.Their vision is constricted to a radius of less than five degrees and/or the ability to recognise a moving object at a distance of one metre.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Near-total visual impairment.
Moderate visual impairment, able to see approximately one metre.

T13.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT13 is for long jumpers with a visual impairment.These long jumpers have a minimal visual impairment, affecting depth perception for the jump take-off.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: A visual impairment, able to see a distance approximately five metres.

T13 (T12/13).descripcion_general: HIGH JUMPT13 is for high jumpers with a visual impairment.They have a restricted field and depth of view affecting take-off precision.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: A visual impairment, able to see a distance approximately five metres.
Moderate visual impairment, able to see approximately one metre.

T20.descripcion_general: T20 is for jumpers who have an intellectual impairment.LONG JUMPLong jumpers may have difficulty when retaining competition information, which affects their ability to alter technique or tactics.TRIPLE JUMPTriple jumpers find it hard to absorb information and apply complex training programmes, especially if it involves multiple instructions.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Athletes with an intellectual impairment.

T33.descripcion_general: T33 is for wheelchair track athletes with movement affected to a high degree in the legs and to moderate degree in the trunk, arms and hands.These athletes have to compensate for the reduced co-ordination of muscle movements.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement moderately affected in the arms and highly affected in the legs.
Movement moderately affected in the trunk from the chest and also in the hands, with movement in the legs highly affected.
Movement in the legs highly affected and moderately affected on one side of the trunk and in one arm.
Movement in the legs highly affected, moderately affected in the trunk and hands and mildly affected in the arms.

T34.descripcion_general: T34 is for wheelchair track athletes with movement affected to a low degree in the trunk and arms, and a high degree affecting the legs.These athletes have to compensate for the reduced co-ordination of muscle movements.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement in the lower trunk and legs affected to a high degree.
Movement in the legs highly affected and mildly affected in the trunk.
Movement mildly affected in the trunk and arms and highly affected in the legs.

T34 (T33/34).descripcion_general: T34 is for wheelchair track athletes with movement affected to a low degree in the trunk and arms, or to a moderate degree in the trunk and arms. All have a high level of impairment affecting the legs.These athletes have to compensate for the reduced co-ordination of muscle movements.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement in the lower trunk and legs affected to a high degree.
Movement in the legs highly affected and mildly affected in the trunk.
Movement mildly affected in the trunk and arms and highly affected in the legs.
Movement moderately affected in the arms and highly affected in the legs.
Movement moderately affected in the trunk from the chest and also in the hands, with movement in the legs highly affected.

T35.descripcion_general: T35 is for runners with a moderately affected in the legs. Some also have movement or coordination affected to a low degree in the trunk and arms.These athletes achieve speed by utilising a short, fast stride.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination affected in the legs to a moderate degree.
Movement in the body and arms mildly affected, with the legs moderately affected.

T36.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT36 is for long jumpers with who have movement affected in all four limbs affected at a low to moderate degree.They compensate for involuntary movements which impacts the start of the run-up, balance, stride pattern and take-off.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination affected in all four limbs to a moderate degree.
Movement and coordination mildly affected in the trunk and legs with the arms moderately affected.
Movement and coordination moderately affected in the trunk and arms and mildly in the legs.

T37.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT37 is for long jumpers with movement moderately affected down one side.They have to compensate for asymmetry in the start, stride and overall power distribution during the take-off.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement moderately affected in one arm and one leg on the same side.
Movement and coordination moderately affected down one side.

T38.descripcion_general: LONG JUMPT38 is for long jumpers with movement affected to a low degree in the lower trunk and legs, down one side or the whole body.They compensate for reduced co-ordination across the stride, arm drive and overall muscle control. Their ability to anticipate the take-off can be affected.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Mildly affected movement or coordination in the lower trunk and legs.
Movement mildly affected in one arm and one leg on the same side.
Movement or coordination mildly affected throughout the whole body.

T38 (T37/38).descripcion_general: T38 is for athletes with movement and coordination affected to a low degree in the lower trunk and legs, whole body or a low to moderate degree down one side.These runners compensate for reduced co-ordination across the stride, arm drive and overall muscle control.  This affects reaction time at the start and across the athlete’s stride pattern.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Mildly affected movement or coordination in the lower trunk and legs.
Movement or coordination mildly affected throughout the whole body.
Movement mildly affected in one arm and one leg on the same side.
Movement moderately affected in one arm and one leg on the same side.

T42.descripcion_general: T42 is for athletes with movement moderately affected in one leg or the absence of limbs.They adjust for asymmetric balance in both the start position and running pattern. They drive from the hips and core to propel themselves.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement and coordination moderately affected in one leg.
Movement and coordination of one leg moderately affected with movement of the knee in that leg highly affected.
Movement or coordination affected in the legs to a moderate degree.
Movement of the hip joint moderately affected.

T43.descripcion_general: T43 is for athletes with movement moderate affected in both lower legs.These runners have unaffected thigh muscles providing good stability through the running cycle.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement and coordination in both lower legs.

T44.descripcion_general: T44 is for athletes with movement affected at a low or moderate degree in one lower leg.They have unaffected knee joints allowing for controlled power delivery but they must focus harder to sense and grip the track surface whilst maintaining good running posture and symmetry.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement and control mildly affected in on lower leg.
Moderately affected movement and coordination in one lower leg.

T44 (T43/44).descripcion_general: T44 is for athletes with movement affected at a low or moderate degree in one or both lower legs.All have difficulty accelerating evenly out of the blocks.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement and control mildly affected in on lower leg.
Moderately affected movement and coordination in one lower leg.
Moderately affected movement and coordination in both lower legs.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement affected to a high degree in the arms.
The absence of both arms.
The absence of both forearms.

F44.descripcion_general: F44 is for field athletes with available movement moderately affected in one lower leg.These athletes have to adjust for asymmetric balance; in the run-up javelin, and across the throwing circle in shot and discus.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement and coordination in one lower leg.

F44 (F43/44).descripcion_general: F44 is for field athletes with available movement moderately affected in one or both lower legs.SHOT PUTShot putters have difficulty focussing on foot placement around the throwing circle, reducing the ability to precisely spin round to release the throwJavelin throwers adapt the run-up and may have difficulty stopping before the no-throw line.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement and coordination in one lower leg.
Moderately affected movement and coordination in both lower legs.

F44 (F42/43/44).descripcion_general: F44 is for field athletes with moderately affected movement in one or both legs.These athletes have to adjust for asymmetric balance; in the run-up for javelin, and across the throwing circle in shot and discus.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement and coordination in one lower leg.
Movement and coordination of one leg moderately affected with movement of the knee in that leg highly affected.
Moderately affected movement and coordination in both lower legs.

F46.descripcion_general: F46 is for field athletes with moderately affected movement in one or both arms or the absence of limbs.These athletes have to transmit power from hips and legs into the throw.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement in both forearms and hands.
The movement in one arm affected to a high degree.
The absence of one arm.

F46 (F45/46).descripcion_general: F46 is for field athletes with moderately affected movement in one or both arms or the absence of limbs.These athletes have to transmit power from hips and legs into the throw.

F51.descripcion_general: F51 is for field athletes who have movement affected to a high degree in the trunk, legs and hands, and low to moderate degree affecting arm movement.CLUBAll throw whilst seated and rely on their shoulders and arm length to generate inertia.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement affected to a high degree in the trunk, legs and hands with movement moderately affected in the arms.

F52.descripcion_general: F52 is for field athletes who have movement affected to a high degree in the trunk, hands and legs, with arm movement affected to a low degree.All throw whilst seated and rely on their shoulders and arm length to generate inertia.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement affected to a high degree in the body and legs, mildly affected in the arms with hand grip and control highly affected.

F52 (F51/52).descripcion_general: F52 is for field athletes who have movement affected to a high degree of in the trunk, hands and legs, with arm movement affected to a low degree.All throw whilst seated and rely on their shoulders and arm length to generate inertia.

Movement affected to a high degree in the trunk, legs and hands with movement moderately affected in the arms.

F53.descripcion_general: F53 is for field athletes who have movement affected to a high degree in the trunk and legs with hand grip affected at a low level.All throw whilst seated and generate inertia using their chest, upper-back and shoulders.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination highly affected throughout the trunk and legs with grip mildly affected in the hands.

F53 (F51/52/53).descripcion_general: F53 is for field athletes who have movement affected to a high degree in the trunk and legs with hand grip affected to a low level.All throw whilst seated and generate inertia using their chest, upper-back and shoulders.

F54.descripcion_general: F54 is for field athletes with movement affected to a high degree in the mid-trunk and legs.All throw whilst seated and generate power through a full range of arm, shoulder and chest movement.

F54 (F53/54).descripcion_general: F54 is for field athletes with movement affected to a high degree in the mid-trunk and legs.All throw whilst seated and generate power through a full range of arm, shoulder and chest movement.

H1-2.descripcion_general: H1-2 is for hand-cyclists that have highly affected movement in the trunk, legs and hands.They generate power using their upper arms and shoulders only and may use devices to assist with grip.A time factoring system is used where the clock runs slower for H1’s than for H2’s.

H1-3.descripcion_general: H1-3 is for hand-cyclists that have highly affected movement in the trunk, legs and hands.All generate power using their upper arms, and shoulders, with some able to use their chest too.  Most are able to grip the handles to transmit power into the bike.A time factoring system is used where the clock runs slowest for H1’s, faster for H2’s and faster again for H3's.

Some riders will use a kneeling position.A time factoring system is used where the clock runs slowest for H1’s and fastest for H5's to determine overall finish position.

Some riders will use a kneeling position.A time factoring system is used where the clock runs slowest for H2’s, faster for H3’s and faster again for H4's.

C1.descripcion_general: C1 riders include those with moderately affected co-ordination, highly affected movement in the lower trunk and legs, or the absence of two limbs.Riders in this class are grouped because they use a seated start, have asymmetric power distribution and have balance issues.

C2.descripcion_general: C2 riders include those with coordination affected at a low level in the arms and body and moderately in the legs, on one side, or the absence of limbs.Riders in this class are grouped together because they use a seated start and have asymmetric power distribution which is low in explosivity.

Grade 1.descripcion_general: Grade 1 riders have moderately affected coordination, highly affected movement in the body, arms and legs, or the absence of limbs.These riders may have balance problems. They perform a walk-only test including large circles with no sharp turns.

Grade 2.descripcion_general: Grade 2 riders have affected movement and coordination from a low to high level in the arms, trunk and legs, or the absence of limbs.These riders mostly walk with short spells of trot. Circling is at a walk, with trotting for straight lines.

They generate power through twisting the body to increase the acceleration of the arms and can achieve a high degree of precision on the putting green.

S1.descripcion_general: S1 is for swimmers with highly affected co-ordination of the trunk, arms and legs.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement affected to a high degree in the body and legs, mildly affected in the arms with hand grip and control highly affected.

S3.descripcion_general: S3 is for swimmers with co-ordination affected to a low degree in the upper-trunk and arms, with the rest of the trunk and legs highly affected, and those with a low overall impairment plus the absence of limbs. These swimmers maintain good body position solely using their arms and upper body.

SB1.descripcion_general: SB1 is for breaststroke swimmers with highly affected co-ordination of the trunk, arms and legs.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement or coordination highly affected throughout the whole body.
Movement and coordination highly affected in the trunk, forearms and legs, with the shoulders moderately affected.

SB2.descripcion_general: SB2 is for breaststroke swimmers with highly affected co-ordination in the trunk, legs and hands, and low reduction in function of the arms, or for those who have a similar impairment but with moderately affected arms.These swimmers move through the water using power generated mostly by their forearms and shoulders.

SB3.descripcion_general: SB3 is for breaststroke swimmers with co-ordination affected to a low degree in the upper-trunk and arms, with the rest of the trunk and legs highly affected, and those with a the absence of limbs.These swimmers maintain good body position solely using their arms and upper body. Most will breathe alternately to keep their head low and bring their hips closer to the surface.Swimmers must show intent to move symmetrically even if their arms and legs are not of even length.

Those with short stature have better balance but are affected to a greater degree by their ability to transmit the power into the water due to the length of their limbs and by higher levels of drag. TT1.descripcion_general: TT1 is for wheelchair players with movement affected to a high degree in the trunk and legs and to a moderate level in the shoulders and arms.With Wheelchair brakes locked-on throughout play, these players hold on to the wheel rim for balance and push their body back to upright using their elbows.  Their bats are strapped to the hand to assist with grip and control.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Moderately affected movement in the shoulders and arms and highly affected from the chest down in the trunk and in the legs.

TT2.descripcion_general: TT2 is for wheelchair players with movement affected to a high degree in the mid-trunk and legs, and to a moderate level in the hands.With Wheelchair brakes locked-on throughout play, these players hold on to the wheel rim for balance and push their body back to upright using their elbows.  Their bats are strapped to the hand to assist with grip and control.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs, with coordination and grip moderately affected in the hands.

TT3.descripcion_general: TT3 Team is for wheelchair doubles players with arm movement affected to a moderate degree with the legs highly affected, or at a high degree to the mid-trunk and legs.These players hold the wheel rim to gain extra reach and maximise arm power. Their wheelchair brakes are locked-on throughout play.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement moderately affected in the arms and highly affected in the legs.
Movement highly affected in the mid-trunk and legs.

TT4.descripcion_general: TT4 is for wheelchair players with trunk and leg movement affected to moderate degree, or to a high degree in the lower trunk and legs.Players stretch their body for sideways movement and use the wheelchair for forwards and backwards movement during play. They increase reach by holding onto the chair.

perfiles_visuales.explicacion_medica: Movement and coordination moderately affected in the trunk and legs.
Movement in the lower trunk and legs affected to a high degree.

TT5.descripcion_general: TT5 is for wheelchair players with leg movement affect to a high degree, or the absence of limbs.These players move both their chair and body to get around the table. Their range of shots is not affected.

The absence of one leg and the other having movement affected to a high degree.
Absence of both legs, just below the hips.

They stand close to the table to increase ball control.

Absence of both forearms and both lower legs.

TT6-7.descripcion_general: TT6-7 Team is for standing doubles players with co-ordination affected to a low level across the whole body, moderately in the arms and legs, or the absence of limbs.These players compensate for slow movement around the table by using a very tactical game. They stand close to the table to increase ball control.

TT6-8.descripcion_general: TT6-8 Team is for standing doubles players with co-ordination affected at a low level, moderately affected in the arms and legs, or the absence of limbs.These players generally have good power and a good range of shots but struggle with balance and speed when faced with varied game play.

TT9-10.descripcion_general: TT9-10 Team is for standing doubles players with movement affected at a low level in one arm or leg, moderately affected in the foot and ankle, or the absence of limbs.These players have full power and a complete range of shots, though their movement around the table and their counter-balancing is slightly affected.Upper-body impairments affect the non-playing side.

Taekwondo.descripcion_general: K44 is for taekwondo fighters with coordination affected to a low-degree on one side, a high degree in one arm or one foot and ankle, or the absence of part of the arms.These fighters adjust for uneven upper body balance by changing stance. Those affected in the ankle have better all round balance but have reduced spring for kicking.

Wheelchair Basketball.descripcion_general: Wheelchair Basketball players are given points based upon their amount of available movement. Players with the least movement score lower than those with more.A Team is made up of five players that can be in any combination as long as their points total does not exceed a total of 14.
